obligation_text: High-risk AI systems that have already been subject to a conformity assessment procedure shall undergo a new conformity assessment procedure in the event of a substantial modification, regardless of whether the modified system is intended to be further distributed or continues to be used by the current deployer.
scoring_text: Knowledge of substantial modification assessment for AI systems, including criteria for determining when modifications trigger re-assessment, conformity assessment procedure selection for modified systems, and change management processes.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of substantial modification assessment for AI systems, including criteria for determining when modifications trigger re-assessment, conformity assessment procedure selection for modified systems, and change management processes.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct AI impact assessments for new and materially modified AI systems, producing documented risk findings with recommended mitigations and residual risk statements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 43(4) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-T-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.627, cosine: 0.709). Secondary: AG-T-006 (AG, cosine: 0.671), OP-K-003 (OP, cosine: 0.597).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.627). Shared vocabulary: [ai, modified, systems]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [assessment, change, conformity, criteria, determining, knowledge] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, conduct, documented, findings, impact, materially] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.865 (P=0.871, R=0.858). Balanced P/R — the 27-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.709 > STS 0.627 (gap +0.081). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, modified, systems] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, modified, systems]. EU AI Act-only: [assessment, change, conformity, criteria, determining, knowledge]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, conduct, documented, findings, impact, materially].
